title: aristida adscensionis subsp. bromoides
intro: Aristida adscensionis subsp. bromoides, also known as the broom grass, is a type of perennial grass native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis, coughs, and colds, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Aristida adscensionis subsp. bromoides include reducing inflammation, soothing sore throats, and relieving congestion. In herbal preparations, the plant is often made into teas, infusions, or decoctions to utilize its medicinal properties, and it is also used in traditional medicine practices to treat various ailments.
